data_sources: National Bureau of Statistics of China

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow latest visible estimate dated 2026-04-09
World Gold Council ETF flow commentary published 2026-04-08 for March 2026 flows
fallbacks_used: CNBC US10YTIP quote page used as a cross-check for real yields because FRED DFII10 was one business day behind the latest market close
Yahoo Finance market-expectations article used to corroborate current Fed hold pricing after the direct FedWatch table was difficult to extract programmatically

macro_environment.summary: Macro conditions are mixed with a modest pro-risk tilt in indices and selective strength in FX. Equities benefit from narrowing credit spreads, falling rate volatility, and strong semiconductor leadership, but a hawkish Fed hold still restrains risk appetite. Commodities are split between a clearly bullish crude backdrop and softer precious-metals conditions, while the strongest currency expression remains a bullish yen driven by Bank of Japan normalization and a narrowing U.S.-Japan rate-gap path.
key_drivers: The Fed remains in a hawkish hold regime, with markets pricing no near-term rate cut and officials emphasizing inflation risks from energy
Credit spreads narrowed while MOVE continued to fall, improving the backdrop for equity index exposure
SOX remained strongly higher, preserving leadership support for Nasdaq and the broader risk complex
The latest EIA report showed a fresh crude inventory draw and the broader Middle East backdrop still points to tight supply conditions
March gold ETF flows remained a major drag on precious metals, and copper turned sharply lower on the latest quote page
The RBA and BoJ still offer relatively constructive domestic policy support for AUD and JPY, while euro-area growth remains subdued

fed_stance.description: Market pricing and current Fed commentary indicate a higher bar for cuts, with traders still expecting no change at the next meeting

real_yields.description: Flat under the methodology's +/-5 bps threshold; CNBC cross-check implied a modest decline from the latest FRED DFII10 reading of 1.93

usd_dxy.description: Flat on the day; DXY was up only 0.05%

vix.description: Balanced risk mood in the 15-20 range, with volatility falling on the day

growth_gdpnow.description: Stable-to-soft growth signal; the latest visible official GDPNow estimate remained 1.3% for 2026 Q1

sox_index.description: Rising strongly on the day and also higher over the past week and month

move_index.description: Falling on the day and also materially lower over the past week and month

copper.description: The latest accessible quote page showed copper lower on the day, signaling softer industrial-metal momentum

oil_inventories.description: EIA reported a 0.9 million barrel draw in U.S. commercial crude inventories to 463.8 million barrels

oil_supply.description: Falling imports, lower total commercial petroleum inventories, and persistent Middle East disruption keep supply conditions tight

gold_etf_flows.description: World Gold Council reported about US$12bn of March outflows, the largest monthly outflow on record

geopolitical_risk.description: Middle East tensions continue to elevate supply and inflation risks even as some acute disruptions have eased
geopolitical_risk.source: Official central-bank commentary and market reporting

ecb_stance.description: The ECB kept rates unchanged in March while emphasizing inflation risks and retaining a tightening bias

rba_stance.description: The RBA raised the cash rate target by 25 bps to 4.10% in March 2026

boj_stance.description: The BoJ kept the overnight call rate around 0.75% while continuing its normalization path

china_pmi.description: China manufacturing PMI rose from 49.0 to 50.4 in March, returning to expansion
china_pmi.source: National Bureau of Statistics of China

instruments.name: Gold
instruments.rationale: Gold remains balanced with a mild downside lean. The Fed is still in a hawkish hold and March ETF flows were sharply negative, which offsets the modest inflation hedge from tighter oil conditions. Because real yields and the dollar are both effectively flat rather than supportive, gold lacks the tailwinds required for a bullish bias.
risks: A decisive drop in real yields would quickly strengthen the bullion setup
Further ETF liquidation would keep spot rallies contained

instruments.name: Silver
instruments.rationale: Silver has the weakest precious-metals setup in the report. A hawkish Fed hold and record March gold ETF outflows are already a drag, and the latest copper signal turned negative rather than supportive. With no meaningful help from real yields, the dollar, or risk mood, silver scores as slightly bearish.
risks: A renewed recovery in industrial metals would improve the silver demand signal
Any turn toward lower real yields would help the broader precious-metals complex

instruments.name: WTI Crude Oil
instruments.rationale: Crude retains the clearest bullish setup among commodities. The EIA reported a fresh crude draw, total commercial petroleum inventories also fell, and the broader geopolitical environment still points to tighter supply conditions. With the dollar flat rather than rising and growth merely soft rather than collapsing, supply remains the dominant driver.
risks: A faster normalization in Middle East supply would reduce the risk premium
A sharp deterioration in growth expectations would weaken the demand side of the crude narrative

instruments.name: S&P 500
instruments.rationale: The S&P 500 has improved to a modestly positive macro backdrop. Narrower credit spreads and a falling VIX offset the drag from a hawkish Fed hold, while real yields and the dollar are not imposing additional pressure. The result is a slight bullish bias rather than a full risk-on signal.
risks: Any renewed widening in high-yield spreads would quickly damage the index backdrop
A shift from falling to rising volatility would weaken the near-term constructive tone

instruments.name: NASDAQ 100
instruments.rationale: Nasdaq keeps a modest positive edge because the most important cross-asset supports remain in place. SOX is still rising strongly and MOVE is still falling, which helps valuation-sensitive growth stocks even while the Fed remains on a hawkish hold. Because real yields are not falling outright, the call stays slight bullish rather than stronger.
risks: A rebound in rate volatility would hurt the tech leadership trade
If semiconductor momentum fades, Nasdaq's relative advantage would shrink

instruments.name: Dow Jones
instruments.rationale: The Dow benefits from a better cyclical mix than it did previously. Credit spreads have narrowed and the 2s10s curve has steepened, both of which help value and cyclicals. The hawkish Fed hold prevents a stronger bullish score, but the overall balance is mildly constructive.
risks: If credit spreads start widening again, the cyclical/value case would weaken quickly
A flatter curve would remove an important support for the Dow composition

instruments.name: Russell 2000
instruments.rationale: Small caps improve the most from the credit signal turning positive. Because high-yield spreads carry double weight for RTY, the latest narrowing matters materially, and curve steepening adds further support. A hawkish Fed hold still limits upside conviction, but the balance now leans slight bullish rather than neutral.
risks: RTY would be vulnerable again if financing conditions tighten or spreads widen
Without a softer Fed or better growth signal, the upside remains limited

instruments.name: Euro FX
instruments.rationale: The euro sits in a near-balanced setup. The ECB still leans hawkish relative to a typical hold, but that support is offset by the Fed's hawkish hold and a subdued euro-area growth backdrop. With the dollar flat rather than decisively weaker and the rate differential roughly stable, the result is neutral with a mild downside lean.
risks: A weaker dollar would quickly improve the euro score
Further deterioration in euro-area growth would push the contract into a clearer bearish signal

instruments.name: Australian Dollar
instruments.rationale: The Australian dollar remains constructive but less cleanly bullish than before because copper has turned lower. That drag is still outweighed by a hawkish RBA, a widened Australia-U.S. policy differential, and a China manufacturing PMI reading back above 50. With risk mood balanced rather than risk-off, AUD keeps a slight bullish bias.
risks: A further deterioration in copper or broader commodity momentum would weaken AUD quickly
Any setback in China data would erode the main cyclical support

instruments.name: Japanese Yen
instruments.rationale: The yen remains the strongest FX expression in the report. The BoJ is still normalizing policy, and that keeps the U.S.-Japan rate gap on a narrowing path that directly supports 6J. Even with risk mood only balanced and the Fed still hawkish, the domestic rates story is strong enough to preserve a bullish yen bias.
risks: A renewed rise in U.S. rates would weaken the narrowing-gap thesis
A broad global risk-on surge could reduce demand for yen exposure
